rationale: Irwin and McClelland (2001) is cited in the methods/results where the authors probe interaction effects using spotlight analyses at different moderator levels (e.g., low vs. high hunger/dieting). This is part of how the paper tests its central moderation hypotheses, so the citation has a clear functional role in the empirical analysis. The citing paper does not critique or extend the 2001 methodological claims; it uses that guidance as an analytic tool in this food-portion context.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Irwin and McClelland's moderated-regression guidance by using spotlight analyses to interpret interactions in its studies. The cited work is used as methodological support for probing moderator effects rather than as a target of theoretical revision.
